_doc: invinoveritas /review verdict <-> ibex GuardrailDecision crosswalk (S215, post safal207 PR #60+#63 merge). Three states map cleanly to authority-equivalent ibex states; one (approve_with_concerns) has no ibex equivalent and is reported as a named gap, not forced.

mappings.rationale: Action proceeds with no recorded caveat on either side. allowed_runtime_use unrestricted on the ibex side; invinoveritas does not gate downstream use.
Both sides mean: do not proceed as-is, the action needs modification before re-submission. Same authority: caller may retry after a bounded change.

Both sides mean: do not proceed, full stop. ibex's HARD_BLOCK restricts allowed_runtime_use to [AUDIT_LOG] only -- matches reject's semantics of recording the refusal, not authorizing any downstream action.

invinoveritas lets the action proceed (same as approve) but attaches a recorded caveat in the verdict body. ibex's enum has no 'proceed, but flagged' state: ALLOW carries no caveat slot, REPAIR/SOFT_BLOCK/HARD_BLOCK all mean don't-proceed-as-is. Forcing this onto ALLOW silently drops the caveat (a real authority difference, not just a label difference); forcing it onto REPAIR silently blocks an action the issuer meant to authorize. Reported as a gap, not a guess -- either the family's authority taxonomy is missing a 'proceed with recorded caveat' state, or invinoveritas should converge this verdict down to ALLOW+annotation. Open question, not resolved here.
